textContent:
Tell me about a time you failed and what have I learned from it.
- During this time, I was leading the publicity and publications committee of our organization in an event. We were in charge of making the pubs and posters that we post on our Facebook page. After the event, I have heard from my members that they were not happy with the way that I handled the delegation of tasks to them. What I did is talk to them individually to hear their concerns better and apologize to them personally.  I then took what they all said and applied it on our next event.

How did you handle a tight deadline?
- During my time in undergrad, I am no stranger to a tight deadline. As sometimes, the professor may give a requirement that is supposed to be passed within the day. Since my professor encourages collaboration within your group, I work with others immediately after the assignment was given to waste no time. I provide my insights to the problem and let my groupmates as well to give their point of view so that everyone can hear each other's opinion. This then leads us to finish a hard problem set right on time. 

Have you gone above and beyond the call of duty?
- When I was tutoring, I encounter students that are curious and willing to learn the material but have a difficulty in understanding it. I then ask them what their interests are and try to relate it to the material. I also provide insights on the material that is outside the scope of their topic but still relevant, to try and keep the student engaged and more knowledgeable about it.

Tell me about a time when you resolved a conflict. 
- During an event in my organization, I assigned two members to work on a pub job that I deemed too difficult for one person to handle. However, come the deadline, I find that these two members were not able to find the time to collaborate and work on their task and individually made their own, making me choose between their work. I talked to them individually about their problems with collaboration and find that they thrive alone rather than working in pairs. I then learned that I will not pair the two members with other people again and just let them work alone. 

Tell me about a data project you have worked on where you encountered a challenging problem. How did you respond?
- My recent project was

textContentHtml:
Tell me about a time you failed and what have I learned from it.

- During this time, I was leading the publicity and publications committee of our organization in an event. We were in charge of making the pubs and posters that we post on our Facebook page. After the event, I have heard from my members that they were not happy with the way that I handled the delegation of tasks to them. What I did is talk to them individually to hear their concerns better and apologize to them personally.  I then took what they all said and applied it on our next event.


How did you handle a tight deadline?

- During my time in undergrad, I am no stranger to a tight deadline. As sometimes, the professor may give a requirement that is supposed to be passed within the day. Since my professor encourages collaboration within your group, I work with others immediately after the assignment was given to waste no time. I provide my insights to the problem and let my groupmates as well to give their point of view so that everyone can hear each other's opinion. This then leads us to finish a hard problem set right on time. 


Have you gone above and beyond the call of duty?

- When I was tutoring, I encounter students that are curious and willing to learn the material but have a difficulty in understanding it. I then ask them what their interests are and try to relate it to the material. I also provide insights on the material that is outside the scope of their topic but still relevant, to try and keep the student engaged and more knowledgeable about it.


Tell me about a time when you resolved a conflict. 

- During an event in my organization, I assigned two members to work on a pub job that I deemed too difficult for one person to handle. However, come the deadline, I find that these two members were not able to find the time to collaborate and work on their task and individually made their own, making me choose between their work. I talked to them individually about their problems with collaboration and find that they thrive alone rather than working in pairs. I then learned that I will not pair the two members with other people again and just let them work alone.
